weekly.headline: Stocks pushed to record highs as earnings stayed strong and the Fed held rates steady.
weekly.happened: U.S. markets rose after a heavy week of company earnings, while the Fed kept its policy rate unchanged at 3.5%–3.75% and investors watched oil-driven inflation risks.
weekly.means_for_you: If you invest for the long term, this week was a reminder that markets can move up even when rates stay high—focus on steady contributions rather than trying to time short-term headlines.
weekly.watch_next_week: Watch for any follow-up Fed speakers after the April meeting, and keep an eye on how oil prices move because it can affect inflation and interest-rate expectations.
